behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names scripts/docker-clean.js, child_process, docker rm, docker compose down, container names postgres-payload-test and mongodb-payload-test, package.json scripts docker:start/docker:clean, and mentions CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md as agent instructions and contributor guide.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to do (create script, remove old script) but not exact code or line-level replacements.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use regex and flexible checks for docker commands and container names, allowing varied implementations.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests run node --check, execute docker-clean.js via subprocess, run pnpm test:unit, pnpm test:types, and docker compose config.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests check for actual execSync/exec usage, container name references, docker compose down presence, and package.json script wiring — a stub would fail the not_stub test requiring 8+ lines and exec call.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All required behaviors (script existence, child_process usage, docker rm, compose down, package.json changes, doc updates) are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with header comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Config-edit, Pass-to-pass) and descriptive names.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and commands are consistent throughout.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file to create (which is the task) and references affected files appropriately without line-level hints.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Gold diff to CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md is limited to replacing docker:stop with docker:clean on the relevant lines.
